metadata.description: Semiconductor industry supply chain dataset. Stage 1 focuses on raw material suppliers feeding foundries (TSMC, Samsung, SK hynix, Intel, GlobalFoundries, SMIC) and IDMs. Schema: companies (nodes), materials (taxonomy), relationships (edges).

tier_definitions.raw_materials_upstream: Feedstock producers: polysilicon, metals, base chemicals
tier_definitions.raw_materials_processed: Finished inputs to fabs: wafers, photoresists, gases, slurries, targets, masks, substrates
tier_definitions.idm: Integrated Device Manufacturers (design + fabricate own chips)
tier_definitions.fabless: Chip designers using foundries (NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Apple, Qualcomm)

relationship_strength_scale.secondary: Confirmed supplier, smaller share or backup
relationship_strength_scale.indirect: Supplies a tier-2 partner who supplies the customer
relationship_strength_scale.estimated: Inferred from market structure, not directly confirmed

metadata.sources_summary: Aggregated from: TSMC 2022 Annual Report supplier disclosure, Mordor Intelligence, MarketsandMarkets, Bernreuter Research (polysilicon), SEMI, company 10-Ks and IR pages, Japan Times, Nikkei, Reuters, Diplomat reporting on helium supply, Thornburg Investment Management ABF report. Specific citations preserved per-record where available.
metadata.notes_v02: Pass 1: deep dive on raw materials. Specialty gases broken out by molecule. Added ALD/CVD precursors, lead frames, bonding wire, ceramics/quartzware, rare earths, compound semi wafers, solder/die-attach. node_type field added to all companies for visualization shape distinction.
metadata.notes_v03: Pass 2: Page 2 (foundries + equipment) full build. ASML sub-supplier chain (Zeiss/Trumpf/Cymer). Big-5 + Japanese specialty + Chinese equipment. Detailed sub-fab locations for TSMC, Samsung, Intel, SK hynix. OSATs ASE/Amkor/JCET/Tongfu/PTI. HBM supply edges. New IDM/fabless: TI, Infineon, ST, Renesas, NXP, ADI, Microchip, ON, MediaTek, Marvell, Sony, Tesla, MS Azure, Google, AWS, Meta, HiSilicon, Alchip, Socionext, Cerebras, Groq.
metadata.notes_v04: Pass 3 polish: node_type reclassification (memory makers -> "memory", OSATs -> "osat", Samsung Foundry -> "foundry"). Added page field (1, 2, both) for filtered visualization. TSMC customer revenue weights (Apple 24%, Nvidia 11%, AMD 7%, etc.). Image sensor material added. Tesla, Sony->Apple, additional HBM/packaging relationships filled in.

recommended_visualizations.page_1: Show all node_type=supplier as circles sized by scale_indicator. Foundries (TSMC, Samsung, Intel, SK hynix, etc) appear as larger central circles. Lines = relationships, weighted by strength.
recommended_visualizations.page_2: Show all node_type=equipment as diamonds (per user spec). Foundries/IDMs/memory as circles with sub-fab map markers. Customers (fabless/OEM) as squares or different shape. Use revenue_weight_pct_2024 from TSMC->customer edges to scale line thickness.

metadata.notes_v05: Pass 4 deepening: (1) EDA tools as new node_type "eda" — Synopsys 31%, Cadence 30%, Siemens EDA 13%, plus China Empyrean. (2) IP cores as new node_type "ip" — ARM, Imagination, Ceva, SiFive, Andes. (3) CoWoS supply chain detail — Nvidia 60%, Broadcom 15%, AMD 11% of TSMC 2026 CoWoS allocation captured as cowos_2026_pct on edges. (4) Full ABF substrate maker landscape — Unimicron, Ibiden, AT&S, Nan Ya PCB, Shinko, Kinsus, SEMCO; plus glass substrate transition (Corning). (5) Structural risk flags (single_source_risk, geographic_risk, export_control_exposed) on companies — makes chokepoints discoverable for visualization. (6) Ajinomoto -> 7 ABF substrate makers IP licensing chokepoint explicit.
metadata.notes_v05_1: Fixed risk-flag dict overwrite bug from v0.5; properly applied single_source_risk/geographic_risk/export_control_exposed.
metadata.notes_v06: Pass 5: HBM equipment, test layer, AI-era customer weights. HBM TC bonders (Hanmi, Hanwha Semitech, BESI, ASMPT, Shinkawa) — captures the 2025 Korean patent war between Hanmi and Hanwha and BESI selection as Micron HBM4 sole supplier. Hybrid bonding (BESI ~67% market). Probe cards (FormFactor, Technoprobe, MJC, JEM, MPI). Test sockets (Yamaichi, Enplas, LEENO, ISC, Yokowo, Smiths, Johnstech). New materials: TC bonder, hybrid bonder, TSV equipment, MR-MUF, probe card, test socket, test handler, memory PHY IP. Five additional fabless: Realtek, Cirrus Logic, Lattice, MaxLinear, Ambarella. Keysight (EDA) and Rambus (IP) added. SK hynix HBM revenue weights to Nvidia (90% of HBM, 27% of SK hynix revenue) — the AI-era datapoint that defines memory landscape.
metadata.notes_v06_1: Polish pass: filled in zero-outbound suppliers (Messer, Avantor, Umicore, Possehl, Enomoto, TBEA, China Rare Earth, Resonac SiC), zero-inbound customers (Vanguard, Powerchip, X-FAB, DB HiTek, Rapidus, Nanya, Tongfu, Huatian, ChipMOS, Hana Micron, Silicon Box), and exercised previously-unused materials (KrF resist, mask blanks, Kr/Xe/O2/NH3/DCS/CHF3/dopant gases, Ti/Co/Au-Pt sputter targets, BT substrate, filtration, La/Dy/Tb rare earths, dry strip).
metadata.notes: v0.6.2 cleanup: removed bad Sony->Samsung CIS edge, downgraded Ceva->Qualcomm to estimated, added geo_cluster_id (metro-area grouping for Page 1 de-clustering), filled missing scale_indicator on Mitsubishi Materials, dropped 5 unused materials.
metadata.cleanup_notes: v0.6.2: removed bad Sony->Samsung CIS edge, downgraded Ceva->Qualcomm to estimated, added geo_cluster_id field on all companies (69 metro + country-fallback clusters), filled missing scale_indicator on Mitsubishi Materials, dropped 5 unused materials.

materials.name: Electronic-Grade Polysilicon
materials.notes: 9N–11N pure silicon feedstock; input to wafer ingot growth (Czochralski / float-zone).

materials.name: 300mm Silicon Wafer
materials.notes: 75% of wafer market value. The substrate every leading-edge chip is built on.

materials.name: 200mm Silicon Wafer
materials.notes: Used for analog, power, sensors, MCUs, and trailing-edge nodes.

materials.name: SOI / Engineered Wafer
materials.notes: Silicon-on-insulator wafers; Soitec is the dominant producer.

materials.name: EUV Photoresist
materials.notes: Light-sensitive polymer for 7nm and below. Japanese firms control ~90%+ of supply.

materials.name: ArF (193nm) Photoresist
materials.notes: ArF immersion held 31.92% of total photoresist market in 2025.

materials.name: KrF (248nm) Photoresist
materials.notes: Used at mature nodes.

materials.name: EUV Photomask Blank
materials.notes: Mo/Si multilayer reflective stack on low-CTE quartz. AGC ~59%, Hoya ~34% market share.

materials.name: Quartz/Chrome Photomask Blank
materials.notes: DUV mask blanks; Hoya, AGC, Shin-Etsu lead.

materials.name: Finished Photomask / Reticle
materials.notes: Patterned mask used to expose wafers. EUV mask sets cost $300K-$10M+ per layer.

materials.name: EUV Pellicle
materials.notes: Thin protective film over reticle. ASML and Mitsui Chemicals are key suppliers.

materials.name: Neon (high-purity)
materials.notes: ~96% of excimer laser gas mix at 193nm. Pre-war, Ukraine supplied 50-70% of global neon.

materials.name: Argon (electronic-grade)
materials.notes: Sputtering, plasma, inert atmosphere. Air-separation byproduct.

materials.name: Krypton (high-purity)
materials.notes: Excimer laser gas mix component.

materials.name: Xenon (high-purity)
materials.notes: Used in EUV gas mixes, etch, ion implant.

materials.name: Helium (high-purity)
materials.notes: Wafer backside cooling at 3nm and below. No substitute. Qatar = ~30% of global supply.

materials.name: Nitrogen (UHP, on-site)
materials.notes: Highest-volume gas in fabs. Produced on-site by air-separation units.

materials.name: Oxygen (UHP, on-site)
materials.notes: Oxidation, ash, on-site air-separation.

materials.name: Specialty Etch Gases (NF3, SF6, WF6, etc.)
materials.notes: Generic catchall for plasma etch and CVD precursor gases. See specific molecules: mat_gas_nf3 (chamber clean), mat_gas_wf6 (W dep), mat_gas_sih4 (Si dep), mat_gas_nh3, mat_gas_dcs, mat_gas_chf3, mat_gas_dopant.

materials.name: Electronic-Grade Hydrofluoric Acid
materials.notes: Etching oxide, cleaning. Stella Chemifa achieves 12N+ purity.

materials.name: UHP Hydrogen Peroxide
materials.notes: Wafer cleaning (RCA/SC1/SC2). Solvay is a leader.

materials.name: UHP Sulfuric Acid
materials.notes: Wafer cleaning, photoresist strip.

materials.name: UHP Isopropyl Alcohol
materials.notes: Drying, cleaning.

materials.name: UHP Ammonium Hydroxide
materials.notes: Cleaning baths.

materials.name: CMP Slurry
materials.notes: Chemical-mechanical planarization slurry. Entegris/CMC controls ~30-40% of slurries.

materials.name: CMP Polishing Pad
materials.notes: DuPont is the dominant pad supplier.

materials.name: Copper Sputtering Target
materials.notes: Interconnect metallization. ~35% of target shipments.

materials.name: Tungsten Sputtering Target
materials.notes: Plug fill, gate stacks.

materials.name: Tantalum Sputtering Target
materials.notes: Diffusion barriers.

materials.name: Titanium Sputtering Target
materials.notes: Liner / barrier.

materials.name: Aluminum Sputtering Target
materials.notes: Older interconnects, pads.

materials.name: Cobalt Sputtering Target
materials.notes: Modern contact / interconnect at advanced nodes.

materials.name: Precious Metal Targets (Au, Pt, Ag)
materials.notes: Specialty / packaging applications.

materials.name: ABF Substrate (Ajinomoto Build-up Film)
materials.notes: Insulating film for FC-BGA packages on CPUs/GPUs/AI chips. Ajinomoto is sole material supplier.

materials.name: Lead Frame
materials.notes: Stamped/etched metal frames for QFN, QFP, etc.

materials.name: Bonding Wire (Au/Cu)
materials.notes: Wire bonding interconnects.

materials.name: Underfill / EMC (Epoxy Mold Compound)
materials.notes: Mold compound for chip encapsulation.

materials.name: Foundry Service (Advanced Node)
materials.notes: Wafer fabrication services at 5/4/3/2nm (TSMC, Samsung, Intel).

materials.name: HBM Memory Supply
materials.notes: High-Bandwidth Memory stacks for AI accelerators.

materials.name: Nitrogen Trifluoride (NF3)
materials.notes: Plasma chamber cleaning. ~$1.2B market 2025. SK Materials ~24% / Kanto Denka ~19% / PERIC, Hyosung. Global demand 30,000+ tonnes/yr.

materials.name: Tungsten Hexafluoride (WF6)
materials.notes: CVD deposition of W interconnects, plug fill, gate stacks. ~7,000-8,000 t/yr global. SK Specialty 2,000 t, PERIC 2,200 t, Kanto Denka 1,400 t, Foosung 900 t. Tungsten supply choked by China export controls (2025).

materials.name: Silane (SiH4)
materials.notes: Pyrophoric Si precursor for CVD/ALD of Si, SiO2, SiN films. $954M market 2024 (semis ~$222M). REC Silicon ~13% leader, then Air Liquide, Linde, China players.

materials.name: Ammonia (electronic grade)
materials.notes: SiN deposition (with SiH4 or DCS), nitridation. Air Products, Linde, Mitsubishi Chemical.

materials.name: Dichlorosilane (SiH2Cl2)
materials.notes: ALD/CVD precursor for SiN, epitaxial Si. Linde, Mitsui Chemicals, Chinese suppliers.

materials.name: Fluorocarbon Gases (CF4, CHF3, C4F8, etc.)
materials.notes: Plasma etch chemistry. Resonac, Kanto Denka, Central Glass.

materials.name: Dopant Gases (B2H6, PH3, AsH3)
materials.notes: Ion implant doping gases. Linde, Air Products, Versum/Merck.

materials.name: Hafnium Precursors (HfCl4, TEMAH, etc.)
materials.notes: High-k gate dielectric (HfO2). 42% of high-k precursor market. ADEKA, Air Liquide, Versum/Merck control ~75%.

materials.name: Zirconium Precursors
materials.notes: ZrO2 high-k, HfZrO ferroelectric memory. Fastest-growing precursor segment.

materials.name: Cobalt Precursors
materials.notes: Modern interconnect liner, contact metallization at advanced nodes.

materials.name: Ruthenium Precursors
materials.notes: Next-gen interconnect, DRAM electrodes. Emerging.

materials.name: Trimethylaluminum (TMA)
materials.notes: Al2O3 ALD (high-k, passivation). Mature high-volume precursor.

materials.name: Titanium Precursors (TiCl4, TDMAT)
materials.notes: TiN barrier layers.

materials.name: Fabricated Quartzware (chambers, tubes, boats)
materials.notes: Quartz furnace tubes, wafer boats, diffusion equipment. Ferrotec, Tosoh SGM, Heraeus, Saint-Gobain quartz feedstock.

materials.name: SiC-coated Graphite Susceptors
materials.notes: Wafer holders for epitaxy and high-temperature CVD. CoorsTek, Toyo Tanso, Morgan Advanced Materials.

materials.name: Process Chamber Ceramics (AlN, SiN, Al2O3)
materials.notes: Heaters, electrostatic chucks, ring components. Kyocera, CoorsTek, NGK Insulators.

materials.name: Process Filtration & Liquid Handling
materials.notes: Ultra-clean filters, fluid handling. Entegris dominant.

materials.name: Cerium Oxide (CeO2)
materials.notes: Ceria-based CMP slurry abrasive (oxide and W polishing). China dominates.

materials.name: Lanthanum Oxide
materials.notes: Optical/specialty applications, some high-k research.

materials.name: Neodymium-Praseodymium (Nd-Pr) Magnets
materials.notes: Permanent magnets in lithography stages, etch tool actuators. China makes 96% of NdFeB magnets.

materials.name: Heavy REE (Dysprosium, Terbium)
materials.notes: High-temp magnets, phosphors. China ~100% global supply. Subject to April 2025 export controls.

materials.name: Silicon Carbide (SiC) Wafer
materials.notes: Power electronics for EV inverters, fast charging. Wolfspeed, II-VI/Coherent, SK Siltron CSS, Resonac, ROHM-SiCrystal.

materials.name: Gallium Nitride (GaN) Substrate
materials.notes: RF power, fast switching power devices. Sumitomo Electric, Mitsubishi Chemical.

materials.name: Indium Phosphide (InP) Wafer
materials.notes: Photonics, RF. Sumitomo Electric, AXT, JX.

materials.name: Solder Balls / Bumps (BGA/Flip-Chip)
materials.notes: Senju Metal, Indium Corp, Heraeus, MK Electron.

materials.name: Die Attach Materials
materials.notes: Henkel, Hitachi Chemical/Resonac, Namics.

materials.name: EUV Lithography Tool (TWINSCAN NXE/EXE)
materials.notes: ASML monopoly. NXE:3800E (low-NA), EXE:5000/5200B (high-NA, 0.55 NA). 12+ month lead time. ~€200-380M per tool. 44 EUV systems shipped 2024.

materials.name: DUV Immersion Lithography (ArF immersion)
materials.notes: ASML dominant; Nikon ~15-20% remaining. 374 DUV scanners shipped by ASML in 2024.

materials.name: DUV Dry Lithography (ArF/KrF/i-line)
materials.notes: ASML, Nikon, Canon. Mature node workhorses. SMEE has ~4% i-line share.

materials.name: Coater/Developer (resist track)
materials.notes: TEL + Screen ~94.5% combined. Pairs with every scanner.

materials.name: Dielectric Etch (CCP etcher)
materials.notes: Lam Research + Applied Materials = 58.9% combined etch share. AMEC entering 5nm via TSMC Nanjing.

materials.name: Conductor / Silicon Etch (ICP etcher)
materials.notes: Lam Kiyo, AMAT Sym3, TEL Tactras. AMEC, NAURA have growing share.

materials.name: Dry Strip / Photoresist Removal
materials.notes: China + Japan tied for top spot. Mattson, NAURA, AMEC in dry strip.

materials.name: Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD/sputter)
materials.notes: Applied Materials Endura platform dominant. ULVAC and Tokki for specialty.

materials.name: Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD/PECVD)
materials.notes: AMAT + Lam ~62.3% of CVD market. NAURA, Piotech rising in China.

materials.name: Atomic Layer Deposition (ALD)
materials.notes: ASMI Pulsar/XP, AMAT Olympia, TEL NT333. Critical for high-k, advanced nodes.

materials.name: Epitaxy Tool (Si/SiGe/SiC)
materials.notes: AMAT Centura, ASMI E2000/Intrepid for advanced. Aixtron/Veeco MOCVD for compound.

materials.name: Vertical Diffusion Furnace / Thermal
materials.notes: Japan ~82% share. KOKUSAI Electric, TEL, Hitachi Kokusai.

materials.name: CMP (Chemical Mechanical Planarization) Tool
materials.notes: Applied Materials Reflexion, EBARA F-REX/CMP-X dominant. KCTech (Korea), Hwatsing (China) emerging.

materials.name: Single-Wafer Cleaner
materials.notes: Japan 63.5% share. Screen Holdings dominant. Lam, ACM Research, NAURA emerging.

materials.name: Batch Cleaner
materials.notes: Japan 73.5% share. TEL, Screen, EBARA.

materials.name: Optical Inspection / Metrology
materials.notes: KLA dominates ~63% overall. Camtek, Onto Innovation, ASML YieldStar.

materials.name: E-beam Inspection / CD-SEM
materials.notes: KLA, Hitachi High-Tech (CD-SEM Japan ~65.5% share), Applied Materials.

materials.name: EUV Mask Inspection
materials.notes: Lasertec near-monopoly on EUV mask blank inspection (ACTIS, MATRICS). NuFlare for mask writers.

materials.name: Ion Implanter
materials.notes: AMAT + Axcelis = full range. Sumitomo Heavy Industries, Nissin (Japan), AIBT (Taiwan), CETC (China).

materials.name: Wafer Dicing/Grinding (DBG)
materials.notes: DISCO Corporation near-monopoly (~70%+) on wafer dicing/grinding.

materials.name: Advanced Packaging Bonder (TC/HB/Flip-Chip)
materials.notes: Besi (hybrid bonding), ASMPT, Kulicke & Soffa for advanced. Critical for HBM stacking.

materials.name: SoC / Logic Test System
materials.notes: Advantest + Teradyne = ~95% combined ATE share. Cohu, Chroma for handlers.

materials.name: Memory Test System
materials.notes: Advantest dominant in memory ATE.

materials.name: Vacuum Pumps (turbo, dry)
materials.notes: EBARA, Pfeiffer, Edwards (Atlas Copco), ULVAC.

materials.name: Foundry Service: Leading-edge (≤3nm/2nm)
materials.notes: TSMC dominates (>90%). Samsung distant 2nd. Intel 18A ramping.

materials.name: Foundry Service: 5/7nm
materials.notes: TSMC dominant; Samsung; SMIC (via DUV multi-patterning).

materials.name: Foundry Service: Mature (≥28nm)
materials.notes: TSMC, GlobalFoundries, UMC, SMIC, Hua Hong, Vanguard, X-FAB.

materials.name: OSAT: Advanced Packaging (CoWoS/InFO/SiP/2.5D)
materials.notes: TSMC in-house 3DFabric dominant for HBM/AI; ASE, Amkor major outsourced.

materials.name: OSAT: Standard Assembly & Test
materials.notes: ASE 44.6%, Amkor 15.2%, JCET 12% — top 10 = 80%+ market.

materials.name: HBM Memory Supply
materials.notes: SK hynix ~50%, Samsung ~40%, Micron emerging — for Nvidia/AMD/Google AI accelerators.

materials.name: CMOS Image Sensor
materials.notes: Sony Semi ~50% market share; primary supplier to Apple/Samsung/Chinese OEMs.

materials.name: EDA Tools (digital + analog design + verification + signoff)
materials.notes: Big-3 oligopoly Synopsys + Cadence + Siemens EDA = 74%+. Full custom + RTL + verification + signoff suites. Used by every fabless and IDM in the world.

materials.name: EDA Simulation & Multi-physics (Ansys, etc)
materials.notes: Synopsys acquired Ansys in 2024 — multi-physics simulation, signal integrity. Adds another ~10% to top-3 share.

materials.name: ARM CPU IP (Cortex-A/M/X, Neoverse)
materials.notes: ARM Holdings — dominant mobile CPU IP. Licensed by Apple, Qualcomm, MediaTek, Nvidia, AWS Graviton, Microsoft Cobalt, NXP, Renesas, etc.

materials.name: GPU IP (Imagination PowerVR, ARM Mali)
materials.notes: Imagination PowerVR and ARM Mali — used in mobile SoCs.

materials.name: DSP IP (Ceva, Synopsys ARC, Cadence Tensilica)
materials.notes: Specialty signal processing IP. Used in RF, audio, imaging.

materials.name: RISC-V CPU IP (SiFive, Andes, Imagination)
materials.notes: Open-ISA RISC-V cores. Renesas + Tenstorrent customers. China bet for ARM independence.

materials.name: Interface/PHY IP (PCIe, USB, DDR, HBM)
materials.notes: Synopsys + Cadence dominant. Hard-IP PHYs for SerDes, DDR, HBM, PCIe.

materials.name: CoWoS 2.5D Advanced Packaging Capacity
materials.notes: TSMC near-monopoly. 2026 capacity targeted ~130k wafers/month; allocated Nvidia ~60%, Broadcom ~15%, AMD ~11%. Critical for all AI accelerators with HBM.

materials.name: Silicon Interposer (CoWoS-S core)
materials.notes: TSV-equipped silicon wafer that connects logic+HBM dies. Made by foundries (TSMC primary; UMC supplies to OSATs for non-CoWoS variants).

materials.name: ABF Substrate (FC-BGA package substrate)
materials.notes: Top-5 (Unimicron, Ibiden, Nan Ya PCB, Shinko, AT&S) ~74% capacity. Built on Ajinomoto ABF film (98% IP licensing chokepoint).

materials.name: BT Substrate (lower-end packaging)
materials.notes: For RF, mobile, mature applications. SEMCO, LG Innotek, Simmtech, Daeduck.

materials.name: Glass Substrate (next-gen packaging)
materials.notes: Pilot at Intel, Samsung. Corning, AGC. Better thermal stability than ABF; 2027+ ramp.

materials.name: Thermal Compression (TC) Bonder
materials.notes: HBM die-stack assembly. TCB applies heat + pressure to bond stacked DRAM. Hanmi was SK hynix monopolist 2017-2024; ASMPT and Hanwha Semitech now diversifying SK hynix supply. BESI selected sole Micron HBM4 TCB supplier. Co-developed Intel + ASMPT 2014.

materials.name: Hybrid Bonding Equipment (bumpless)
materials.notes: Bumpless wafer-to-wafer or die-to-wafer bonding for ultra-fine pitch. Required for HBM 16-Hi+ and TSMC SoIC. BESI dominant ~67% market. Cumulative >100 hybrid bonders shipped 2021-2024. 100nm precision shipped 2024; 50nm targeted 2025.

materials.name: MR-MUF / TC-NCF Underfill Material
materials.notes: SK hynix MR-MUF (Mass Reflow + Molded Underfill) co-developed with NAMICS — proprietary, key thermal differentiator vs Samsung/Micron's TC-NCF (Non-Conductive Film). Driving SK hynix HBM yield/perf advantage.

materials.name: Probe Card (wafer test)
materials.notes: MEMS probe cards dominant (~73% subseg). $3.8B market 2024. Top 5 (FormFactor, Technoprobe, MJC, JEM, MPI) hold 64-73%. Critical wafer-level test interface.

materials.name: Test & Burn-in Socket
materials.notes: Cohu ~19%, Smiths Interconnect ~16%. Test contactor between handler and DUT, includes consumable probe pins. ~$1.8B market 2024.

materials.name: Test Handler (pick-and-place / strip / turret)
materials.notes: Cohu market leader. Sub-segments: pick-and-place, gravity feed, strip, turret. Distinct from ATE testers (Advantest/Teradyne) which generate the test signals.

materials.name: Memory Subsystem PHY/Controller IP
materials.notes: DDR5/HBM/CXL PHY and controller IP. Rambus and Synopsys lead.

companies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
companies.notes: Japan's largest chemical company. World #1 silicon wafer supplier and a top-3 EUV photoresist maker. Investing $500M in new Gunma plant for lithography materials.

companies.name: SUMCO
companies.notes: World #2 silicon wafer producer. JV history of Sumitomo + Mitsubishi Materials wafer businesses.

companies.name: GlobalWafers
companies.notes: World #3 wafer producer; acquired SunEdison Semiconductor 2016. Building first new US 300mm wafer fab in 20 years under CHIPS Act.

companies.name: Siltronic AG
companies.notes: Spun off from Wacker Chemie in 2015.

companies.name: SK Siltron
companies.notes: Part of SK Group. Direct supply ties with Samsung, SK hynix, TSMC, Intel, NVIDIA, Qualcomm.

companies.name: Soitec
companies.notes: Dominant SOI substrate producer; FD-SOI used by GlobalFoundries, RF-SOI in mobile front-end modules.

companies.name: Hemlock Semiconductor
companies.notes: JV originally Dow Corning + Shin-Etsu. $325M federal grant for new MI facility (2026).

companies.name: Tokuyama Corporation
companies.notes: ¥30B Shunan plant expansion for sub-3nm purification (2024).

companies.name: JSR Corporation
companies.notes: Pioneer in chemically amplified resists. Major TSMC partner. Inpria acquisition gave them metal-oxide EUV resist leadership.

companies.name: Dongjin Semichem
companies.notes: First Korean photoresist producer; fourth globally. Critical for Samsung and SK hynix supply diversification.

companies.name: Merck KGaA / EMD Electronics
companies.notes: Acquired Versum Materials (2019) and AZ Electronic Materials. Broad portfolio across the fab.

companies.name: Linde plc
companies.notes: World's largest industrial gases company (post Praxair-Linde merger). 4 firms (Linde, Air Liquide, Air Products, Taiyo Nippon Sanso) control >90% of electronics specialty gases globally.

companies.name: Taiyo Nippon Sanso (Nippon Sanso Holdings)
companies.notes: Major Asian electronics gases supplier; named directly in TSMC supply list.

companies.name: SK Materials
companies.notes: Part of SK Group; major NF3 producer.

companies.name: Stella Chemifa
companies.notes: World leader in ultra-high-purity HF (12N+ achieved). Direct supply to Japanese fabs and Arizona/Texas fabs.

companies.name: Morita Chemical
companies.notes: Japan's leading fluorine compound producer. Supplies Sony Semiconductor and Renesas.

companies.name: Kanto Chemical
companies.notes: Named in TSMC's chemicals supplier list.

companies.name: Honeywell International
companies.notes: Major HF and sputter target producer; recent supply deals with Intel and TSMC Arizona.

companies.name: Soulbrain
companies.notes: Critical Korean HF supplier for Samsung and SK hynix; expanded after 2019 Japan export controls.

companies.name: JX Advanced Metals / JX Nippon Mining & Metals
Materion Corporation
companies.notes: Acquired Heraeus Precious Metals' sputter target business. 2,500+ MT/yr sputter materials.

companies.name: Plansee Group
companies.notes: Specialist in molybdenum and tungsten targets.

companies.name: Photronics
companies.notes: One of only 4 companies globally capable of EUV mask production. Partners with IBM on 2nm masks. Owns 50.01% of PDMC Xiamen.

companies.name: Dai Nippon Printing (DNP)
companies.notes: Often cited as overall photomask leader; delivering EUV masks for sub-2nm.

companies.name: Toppan / Tekscend Photomask
companies.notes: Tekscend Photomask runs 8 mfg sites worldwide including Dresden expansion.

companies.name: Ajinomoto Fine-Techno
companies.notes: Sole producer of ABF (Ajinomoto Build-up Film). Critical material for FC-BGA packaging — all high-end CPU, GPU, AI accelerator chips.

companies.name: Unimicron Technology
companies.notes: World's largest ABF substrate manufacturer. NVIDIA, Apple, AMD customers.

companies.name: Ibiden
companies.notes: Long-time Intel substrate partner. Major capex subsidized by Intel/AMD/NVIDIA.

companies.name: Mitsui Chemicals (specialty gas/pellicle)
companies.notes: Mitsui Chem already in dataset for pellicles; expanded role: announced exit from NF3 business by March 2026 (Shimonoseki Mitsui Chemicals subsidiary). Still supplies DCS and related precursors.

companies.name: 3M Company
companies.notes: Named in TSMC 2022 CMP materials supplier list.

companies.name: TSMC (Taiwan Semiconductor)
